content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(91h) ;J:.!:.!-5660 • F:1x (916} ;J:.!'..!•08Hh 
Ms. Lori Herbel 
7518 Ventura Avenue 
Yucca Valley, CA 92284 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-273 
Dear Ms. Herbel: 
November 30, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding Government Code Section I 090, et 
seq.1 Please note that we do not advise on any other area oflaw, including Public Contract Code or 
common law conflicts of interest. We are also not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re 
Oglesby ( 1975) l FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and 
accurate. 
In regard to our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the San Bernardino 
County District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.l(c)(3).) We did not receive 
a written response from either entity. (Section 1097. I ( c )( 4 ). ) We are also required to advise you 
that, for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section I 097.1 (c)(S).) 
QUESTION 
Does your position on the Yucca Valley Measure Y and Measure Z Oversight Commission 
pose a Section I 090 problem in light of salary you have previously received as a contract instructor 
from the Town's community service department? 
CONCLUSION 
Section 1090 does not impact your participation as a member of the advisory commission 
because there is no indication that actions by the advisory commission involve a contract you may 
enter into as an instructor for Yucca Valley. 
FACTS 
You were appointed by the Yucca Valley Town Council to their Measure Y and Z 
1 Government Code Sections 1090 through 1097.5. All statutory references are to the Government Code, 
unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 
through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of 
the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-17-273 
Page No. 2 
Oversight Commission in April 2017. The Oversight Commission serves as an advisory 
commission to the Yucca Valley Town Council and members do not receive any type of 
compensation, salary, or stipend. Both Measure Y and Z established a temporary half-percent 
general transactions and use tax. Measure Y funds are for essential services, and Measure Z funds 
are for a sewer assessment reduction. Additionally, for the past six years, you have received 
compensation as a contract instructor for a dog training course through the Town's Community 
Service Department. 
ANALYSIS 
Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the official has 
a financial interest" (People v. Honig, (1996) 48 Cal. App. 411' 289, 333), and officials are deemed to 
have a financial interest in a contract if they might profit from it in any way. (Id.) Although Section 
l 090 nowhere specifically defines the term "financial interest," case law and Attorney General 
Opinions state that prohibited financial interests may be indirect as well as direct, and may involve 
financial losses, or the possibility oflosses, as well as the prospect of pecuniary gain. (Thomson v. 
Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 645, 651-652; see also People v. Vallerga (1977) 67 Cal.App.3d 847, 
867, fu. 5; Teny v. Bender (1956) 143 Cal.App.2d 198, 207-208; People v. Darby (1952) 114 
Cal.App.2d 412, 431-432; 85 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 34, 36-38 (2002)). 
As a contract instructor for a dog training course through the Town's Community Service 
Department, you would have a financial interest for the purposes of Section 1090. However, based 
on the facts provided, we do not find evidence that the contract for your position as an instructor for 
the dog training course would come before the Oversight Commission. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (9 l 6) 322-5660. 
Sincerely, 
Brian G. Lau 
Assistant General Counsel 
By: 
 
Senior Counsel, Legal Division 
ZWN:jgl

Does your position on the Yucca Valley Measure Y and Measure Z Oversight Commission 
pose a Section I 090 problem in light of salary you have previously received as a contract instructor 
from the Town's community service department?

because there is no indication that actions by the advisory commission involve a contract you may 
enter into as an instructor for Yucca Valley.

CONCLUSION: Section 1090 does not impact your participation as a member of the advisory commission
